mnemonics: NATURAL (Neural networks are Adaptable, Tolerant of noise, Robust, Universal function approximators, Learn complex patterns, Automate feature extraction)
keyPoints: ANNs can learn complex, non-linear relationships from data.
They are robust to noise and can generalize well to unseen data.
ANNs can automatically extract features, reducing the need for manual feature engineering.
They require large amounts of data and computational power for training.
ANNs are often considered 'black boxes' due to their lack of interpretability.
They can be prone to overfitting if not properly regularized.
commonMistakes: Using ANNs for simple problems where a simpler model would suffice.
Not using enough data to train the network, leading to poor performance.
Failing to properly preprocess data (e.g., normalization).
Using an overly complex architecture for the problem, increasing risk of overfitting.
Ignoring the computational cost and time required for training and tuning.
